page.content: In the 3rd Quarter of 2023, Tencent Holdings reported cybersecurity incidents categorized by Incident Type, with Data Breaches totaling 150 incidents classified as High severity, requiring a Response Time of 24 hours, incurring a Cost Impact of $5M, a Recovery Time of 1 week, and a Financial Impact of $10M, alongside Insurance Claims amounting to $200M and Litigation Costs of N/A. Malware Attacks totaled 80 incidents at Medium severity, with a 12-hour Response Time, $2M Cost Impact, 3-day Recovery Time, Financial Impact of $4M, Insurance Claims of $150M, and Litigation Costs of N/A. Phishing Attempts reached 300 incidents at Low severity, requiring a 2-hour Response Time, with a $1M Cost Impact, 1-day Recovery Time, Financial Impact of $2M, Insurance Claims of $50M, and Litigation Costs of N/A. DDoS Attacks accounted for 50 Critical severity incidents with a Response Time of 30 minutes, an $8M Cost Impact, a Recovery Time of 5 days, a Financial Impact of $12M, Insurance Claims of $75M, and Litigation Costs of N/A.

page.content: The Total Incidents reached 580, with all other metrics listed as N/A. In Data Center Utilization for Q32023, Colocation had a Total Capacity of 5000 kW and 85% Utilization Rate, Cloud Services 8000 kW at 90% Utilization, Hybrid Solutions 6000 kW at 78% Utilization, and Edge Computing 3000 kW at 75% Utilization, leading to a Total Capacity of 22000 kW and an overall Utilization Rate of 82%.

page.content: Revenue was reported at $10,000 AUD Monthly Cost and $120,000 AUD Annual Cost, with projected growth at 10%, an Investment of $500,000 AUD, and Risk Assessment rated Low.
